Description: Memo is received, time/date stamped and acknowledged in Director's office by the Office Aide
Office Aide sends to the Director who minutes/instructs accordingly to officer responsible for related task
Office Aide dispatches minuted memo to officers responsible OR Office Manager/Officer responsible prepares draft memo and sends draft to Director for review

Director reviews, amends if need be and returns to Office Manager/officer responsible for production of final draft OR Director finalises, signs and dispatches electronically while copying office email to constitute file copy for archiving by Office Manager and Office Aide
Office Aide dispatches to destination while ensuring acknowledgement
Memo is reviewed for receipt by Office Manager for hard copies OR Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
Memo without necessary supporting documents/wrongly transmitted is returned to Client  Directorate OR Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
Memo with all supporting documents is received and acknowledged in incoming register by Office Aide or electronically by Director
Office Aide submits memo to Director for action Director acts on request/minutes memo to appropriate DCP staff/destination Director when acting on request electronically, copies DCP office email
Office Aide submits memo to Director for action Director acts on request/minutes memo to appropriate DCP staff/destination Director when acting on request electronically, copies DCP office email
Office Aide archives hard/electronic copy
Office Aide dispatches duly registered memo to destination
Office Aide delivers memo to destination and obtains acknowledgement in outgoing registe
Director receives from PPO Protocol soft or hard copy of draft Note Verbale withsupporting documents where necessary
Director reviews and sends back to PPO Protocol for production of final hard copy while directing Office Manager to provide reference number
Director receives final version for initialing
Director dispatches initialed Note Verbale to Protocol through Office Aide
Initialed Note Verbale is dispatched to Protocol Division
Director proposes dates for statutory meetings to Management for approval
Director amends and returns for production of final draft and production of cover memo by Office Manager
Office Manager submits final correpondence and relevant cover memo to Director for signature
Director sends electronic version of correspondence to Commissioner for review
Upon return of CGAC amendments, Director adjust as necessary
Office Manager prepares cover memo to accompany final versions to be dispatched of correspondence to CGAC
Director signs cover memo, while checking again accompanying final versions
Office Aide dispatches to CGAC/destination

Director proposes dates for Statutory Meetings to Management for approval/receives dates from Management Once dates are received, Director informs DCP colleagues and prepares draft correspondence for Management to inform all Community Institutions Director instructs HOD Conference to approach DCP colleagues for expression of needs Director instructs PPO Protocol to provide list of recommended hotels and negotiated rates

Director convenes initial preparation meeting with HODs and directs prepartions of all deliverables, including any required liaison with support directorate and deliverables from Director's Office itself
Director reviews all deliverables for adjustments where necessary/acts on deliverables and/forwards deliverables to Management
Director receives further instructions from Management and hands down further instructions to HODs for adjustment where necessary and completion of all deliverables
Director invites Management to conduct final check of facilities and arrangements/obtains final approval for implemetion of all deliverables/release of all document
While meeting/event is ongoing Director monitors smooth functioning of all meeting pillars (payments, bills, documentation, accreditation, signature of final reports, etc.)
Director supervises the dismantling of meeting
Director receives analysis of feedback and assessment forms, and reviews DCP procedures accordingly
Director receives instructions from Management to prepare contributions
Director requests Director's Office support staff to provide previous versions where it exists/prepare preliminary draft
Director requests elements from divisions where necessary in electronic version
Director directs  support staff to merge all elements and prepare second preliminary draft
Director reviews second preliminary draft and dispatches to support staff/ffice Manager/Documentation Division for formatting
Director receives formatted version
Director forwards DCP contribution to appropriate quarters (electronic version or hard copy)

Director receives instruction from Management/Technical Directorate OR Director requests draft concept note/terms of reference from relevant DCP staff in implementation of approved budget/activity plan
Director reviews draft received and instructs relevant DCP staff to prepare final version
Director dispatches completed document to appropriate quarters
Director instructs support staff to prepare relevant background information, notice of meeting, draft agenda and work program
Director reviews preliminary background information and supporting document, amending where necessary or issuing instruction to DCP staff for finalisation
Director directs Office Manager to dispatch notice of meeting, agenda, work program and any supporting documents
Director instructs support staff to prepare annotated agenda for Director’s own use
Director appoints meeting rapporteur and moderates meeting
Director disseminates the report of meeting/summary of conclusions/reminder of intructions to relevant DCP staff
Director receives approved draft agenda from Management
Director sends approved agenda to support staff for insertion of details into status of documents
Director instructs support staff to continuously adjust status of documents in line with President’s instructions
Director instructs support staff to format and upload documents as against President’s approval of agenda items Director continues to release documents as upload is completed by  support staff
Director remains available  to instruct staff for replacement/removal of any uploaded document
Director checks that system has automatically archived all documents
Conference Division through DCP requests for the activities for the quarter from Finance Directorate
HOD assigned a Conference Assistant to extract detailed information and produce preliminary calendar
HOD allocates each Conference Assistance aspecific directorate(s)
Each Conference Assistant to liaise with his allocated directorate to confirm the meetings in the quarterly calendar and update with logistics (venue/number of days/number of participants)
Assigned Conference Assistant undertakes check at end of every month and updates calendar when necessary. Collation continues
Conference Assistants send information collected to officer in charge of collation for review and dispatch to HOD who will perform final check and dispatch to DCP
Issuance of the calendar of events
HOD received instruction from DCP toprepare budget for the activity
Conference Assistant contacts other DCP divisions/support directorates involved, also Client Directorate, to obtain all details necessary for budgetpreparation.
Conference Assistant prepares the draft budget and submits to HOD for vetting
HOD sends the prepared budget to DCP for onward transmission to Technical Directorate
Logistics budget available
Approved memo/meeting request form received from DCP by HOD
HOD minutes the meeting request to Conference Assistant in charge for necessary logistics preparation
Conference Assistant completes the logistics request form as against approved budget and submits to DCP through the HOD
DCP approves and sends to Admin Directorate
Logistics requested received from General Admin and deployed by Conference Assistant assigned to the meeting/element of meeting
Logistics arrangement concluded
HOD requests menu and meal plan from identified caterer(s)
Assigned Conference Assistant proposes menu choice to HOD for validation

HOD with participation of Conference Assistant calls for meeting with caterer to discuss identified service points, timings, food distribution, presentation, packaging, number of wait staff, waste disposal, etc.
Conference Assistant checks compliance with contract and menu at delivery. Conference Assistant continues surpervision until the end of the event.
Conference Assistant submits meeting assessment form to Client Directorate and receives completed form for dispatch to HOD and onward transmission to DCP
Conference Assistant submits meeting feedback form to HOD and receives completed form for dispatch to HOD for onward transmission to DCP
Caterer deployed and evaluated
HOD with participation of Reprographic/Documentation Officer and Conference Assistant(s) convenes meeting with designated adhoc staff to assign duties and positions.
Dedicated refresher for all ushers is conducted to review ushering checklist by Senior Conference Assistant/HOD
Supervising staff monitors performance of adhoc staff
Supervising Officer completes contract evaluation form and submits to the HOD for onward transmission to DCP
Adhoc staff deployed and evaluated
Approved memo/meetingrequest form received from DCP by HOD
HOD assigns to officer in Charge of logistics planning for external meetings. Officer analyses request and compiles meeting logistics needs and services
Officer dispatched emails to designated service provider which reflects the compilation of meeting logistics needs and other relevant details. Email is copied to HOD and DCP.
As indicated by officer, HOD notifies DCP of any request to be made to resident reps. or national office/ECOWAS entities
Officer assesses for comformity with approval and negotiates, if necessary, until agreement is reached.
Officer through the HOD requests confirmation from client directorate and notifies service provider accordingly while also indicating the date of the meeting after conference division arrival.
Logistics Concluded

HOD/Conference Assistant leads inspection and walk through of meeting facilities. Identification and allocation of offices and work space, preparation of office space directory, labelling and room tagging.
HOD/Conference Assistant sketches layout reflecting set-up & seating arrangement and shares with service provider for appropriate set-up to be done

Conference Assistant prepares and places nameplates to reflect approved participants and order of precedence as well as seating plan. Conference Assistant guides hostesses toundertake labelling of seats and familiarization with designated seating areas
Conference Assistant guides  hostesses to place writing kits on tables and any other folders/documents as indicated by DCP
Conference Assistant informs HOD to invite Meeting Coordinator/DCP/Management for final inspection
Set-up concluded
HOD/Reproduction Officer leads inspection and walk through of meeting logistics items. Identification and allocation of working tools
HOD/Reproduction Officer sketches layout reflecting set-up and shares with service provider for appropriate set-up to be done.
Reproduction Officer/Assistant and technicians install and test run office machines at the secretariat and others offices spaces.
Reproduction Officer/Assistant and technicians reproduce and package documents. Reproduction Officer checks packed folder and submits to DCP/Coordinator for last vetting.
Actor: Reproduction Officer, Reproduction Assistant, Technicians, DCP Staff

Description: Reproduction Officer/Assistant delivers package documents to HOD Protocol/Assistant for distribution
Meetings  equipment/documents available
Reproduction Officer designs badges and seeks for management approval through DCP
Reproduction Officer/Assistant receives approved list of participants for accreditation and produces badges accordingly
Reproduction Officer/Assistant produces badges, groups them according to the approved list and deliver to DCP
DCP vets delivered badges and sends to Head of Protocol for distribution
Badges produced and delivered
HOD Conference/Assistant identifies the place for SIE set-up and shares with the provider
HOD Conference/Assistant follows on the set-up of the SIE arrangement in the hall
HOD Conference/Assistant arrangesv chairs in the booths according to the approved type of language team and also makes provision for extra seat for rotational purposes
HOD Conference/Assistant informs the Language Coordinator when the set-up ends
SIE space identified
Approved memo/meeting request form received from DCP by HOD
HOD minutes the meetin request to conference assistant in charge for creation of virtual links & also to Conference Assistant in charge of other logistics arrangement
Conference Assistant in charge of the link creation contacts the Client Directorate for other necessary information.
Conference Assistant completes the logistics request form as against approved budget and submits to DCP through the HOD
Conference Assistant in charge of the link creation receives from the Client Directorate accurate information for the link setup.
DCP approves the logistics request form and sends to General Admin Directorate.  Conference Assistant follows up on logistics deployement
Logistics concluded
Conference Assistant designs the meeting link according to the received information
Conference Assistant forwards the created link to Client Directorate and in copy to DCP/HOD/other support divisions.
Conference Assistant forwards the created meeting information to the Conference Assistant in charge of creation of dashboard who will complete and dispatch to HOD
Conference Assistant forward the created meeting information to Client Directorate and in copy to DCP/HOD/other support divisions.
Conference Assistant in charge of logistics contacts the service provider with the refreshment dispatch plan for its implementation.
Virtual meeting host from conference Division completes contract evaluation form, submits to HOD for onward transmission to DCP
Meeting link available
Approved memo/meeting request form received from DCP by HOD
HOD assigns to officer in charge of logistics planning for external meetings. Officer analyses request and compiles meeting logistics needs and services
Officer dispatched emails to designated service provider which reflects compilation of meeting logistics needs and other relevant details. Email is copied to HOD and DCP.
As indicated by Officer, HOD notifies DCP of any request to be made to resident reps. or national office/ECOWAS entities
Officer assesses for comformity with approval and negotiates, if necessary, until agreement is reached.
Officer through the HOD request confirmation from Client Directorate and notifies service provider accordingly while also indicating the date of the meeting after conference division arrival.
Logistics concluded
Conference Officer inspects requested meeting facilities including spill over rooms. Also assures that the meeting designed link were dispatched to all participants.

Conference Officers in collaboration with ITS staff proceed to the set-up of meeting facilities (hall/spill over rooms/secretariat items/internet connectivity/refreshment arrangement and all ad-hoc staff).

Conference Officer proceeds to the repartition of participants into the various meeting rooms (hall/spill over rooms) and seek for DCP approval, then past accordingly at the door of each meeting rooms.
Conference Officer reports to DCP/coordinator on the set-up facilities and invites for inspection.
DCP approves the set-up and validates for implementation carried out by the Conference Officer.
Conference Officer completes contract evaluation form, submits to the HOD for onward transmission to DCP
Meeting link and set-up deployed
Conference Assistant checks the validity of the meetings with the Client Directorates and prepares a draft “Events of the week”
Conference Assistant prepares the draft “Events of the week” including the assigned conference contact for each meeting and sends to HOD for vetting
HOD sends the draft “Events of the week” to DCP for approval and dispatch to communication directorate for publication
“Events of the week” available
Conference Assistant receives requested SIM cards according to the staff list for the meeting
Conference Assistant separates management telephone list from the general list
Conference Assistant allocates telephone numbers to each staff on the list and submits to HOD/Coordinator for verification
HOD/Conference Assistant hands over to HOD/Protocol Assistant for dispatch
HOD/Conference Assistant with ITS staff facilitates the acquisition of modems/routers and recharges token
Conference/Reproduction Assistant receives the used modems/routers from ITS staff for safe keeping at the end of the meeting
SIM cards/data token/routers available
Conference staff inspects all required infrastructure allocated for the meeting
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff labels all allocated meeting spaces including spill over rooms and assigns participants to each meeting rooms
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff in collaboration with the hotel sets up the hall according to ECOWAS standard
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff/ITS staff checks through all digital connection in all meetings halls
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: HOD/conference staff invites DCP/Coordinator to validate the set-up.
Actor: HOD, Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff (Reproduction Assistant) designs the signage including the list of participants (spill over rooms) and pastes them where necessary.
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Physical & virtual set-up done
Conference staff invites all contracted service providers to the venue for the installation of requested logistics
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff conducts a meeting with each service provider on ECOWAS standard servicing
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Service providers install all equipment under the supervision of conference staff and ITS staff
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference/ITS staff proceeds on the dry run for all equipments including dry run for virtual meetings
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff invites meeting coordinator for briefing.
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff may receive further instructions from the Coordinator
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Set-up of working logistics done
Reproduction staff downloads received meeting documents from DCP
Actor: Reproduction Officer, Reproduction Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Reproduction staff edits and formats the received document according to ECOWAS standards
Reproduction staff reproduces the received documents according to directives from DCP
Reproduction staff collates and packages documents for distribution
Reproduction staff send sbound document and electronic version to Reprographic Officer for vetting
Actor: Reproduction Officer, Reproduction Assistant, Reprograhic Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Reprographic Officer sends packed folder to DCP for final vetting and approval for distribution. Folders are handed over to the HOD Protocol/protocol assistants
Actor: Reproduction Officer, Reproduction Assistant, Reprograhic Officer

Description: Meeting folders ready and dispatched
Conference Assistant man his duty station in the hall
Conference Assistant supervises support staff and monitors smooth running of the meeting
Conference Assistant follows up on F&B set-up according to ECOWAS standard
Conference Assistant distributes meal tickets to all eligible participants and informs DCP/Coordinator accordingly
Conference Assistant reviews distribution quality and quantity of F&B
Conference Assistant monitors the conclusion of F&B service (waste disposal, management of leftovers, etc.)
Meeting well coordinated
Conference staff requests the bills from the service providers
Actor: Conference Officer, Conference Assistant, DCP Staff

Description: Conference staff vets bills in line with the approved budget and submits to DCP/Coordinator for approval and onward transmission for payment
Conference staff fills feedback assessment form and submits to HOD Conference for onward transmission to DCP
Conference staff fills the service satisfaction form and submits to HOD for review and signature
Conference staff ushers the service provider to the accountant for payment
Vetted bills duly submitted for payment
Conference Assistant takes a random number of feedback forms as a basis for analysing the service
Conference Assistant analyses the filled feedback forms
Conference Assistant presents and hands over to HOD for onward transmission to DCP
HOD prepares a quality control report for DCP
SWOT analysis been concluded
Conference/reproduction assistants initiate clearance of all conference materials by instructing adhoc staff on the collection of all leftover documents and meeting materials
Conference/reproduction assistants with the help of support staff clear all used conference materials from all meeting spaces
Reproduction/conference assistants ensure proper shredding of all leftover documents
Reprographic officer/conference assistants ensure that all hired equipments are released to the service providers
Meeting space cleared
Receipt of interpretation service request minuted by DCP to Language Coordinator (LC)
LC shall minute request to the Language Assistant (LA) for initial assessment

LA shall assess the validity of the interpretation service request & adequacy of client’s supporting documents (budget adequacy, PR number, working documents, venue, working languages, and the number of participants, need for SIE, etc.)
LA shall submit the assessed request to LC
When the request meets requirements, it is validated by LC

In the event that for cogent reasons interpretation cannot be provided (unavailability of staff interpreters and inadequacy of budget and other relevant documents/information), the LC shall inform DCP for appropriate action to be passed on to the Client Directorate
When the interpretation service request is validated by LC or returned to the client because of in-house staff unavailability and lack of adequate documentation to recruit freelancers
Based on the interpretation service request, duly approved by DCP, LC shall determine language combination, team strengths and SIE or virtual meeting needs
LC shall check availability of staff interpreters and assign a team accordingly if they are available

LA shall acknowledge receipt of the request, inform the client directorate about the action taken, on the instruction of LC give the team leader's contact, request for working documents and any other information

When staff interpreters are not available and there are adequate budgetary provisions, LC shall propose a team of freelancers (as well as SIE if need be) to DCP for approval based on proximity criteria
Once DCP's approval is obtained, LC shall instruct LA to send out availability requests to freelancers (and proforma invoice requests to SIE providers if need be)
LC shall instruct LA to send out confirmations of offers to freelancers who confirmed their availability. For those who turned down the offer, new names shall be submitted by LC to DCP.
LA shall generate the appropriate contracts, based on team size and language combination (including retour) and submit them to LC
LC shall check the contracts before submitting them to DCP
Upon receipt of the signed contracts from DCP, LC shall direct LA to email scanned copies to freelance interpreters for signature and endorsed proforma invoice to SIE providers
LA shall send working documents (or give the username and password to access ECODOCS as far as statutory meetings are concerned) and useful links to interpreters concerned
In case it is a virtual meeting, LA shall obtain the meeting link from Conference Section and send it to interpreters
LA shall receive and file the signed contracts and endorsed proforma invoice from freelance interpreters and SIE provider, respectively
When freelance interpreters (and SIE providers) receive their signed contracts or when staff members are assigned to a meeting in the language schedule
On LC’s instructions, LA shall prepare the Language Schedule where staff assignments are indicated and email it to line managers, staff interpreters and translators and other DCP Divisions
Once notified of a meeting outside their duty station, staff interpreters shall make their TVM requests on ECOLINK

On LC’s instructions, LA shall prepare ticket request and send it back to him/her for proper verification. The ticket request shall be sent to DCP who shall sign and forward it to Travel Unit for issuance of tickets.

Should ECOWAS rent a mobile SIE from a supplier, it shall be installed no later than the eve of the meeting, shall be funtional and placed in a position where a clear view of the high table/projection screen is provided. In addition, it shall be compatible with the virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms.

LC shall appoint team leaders for meetings. The most senior staff interpreter shall be the de facto Team Leader. In event that there is no staff interpreter on the team, a freelance interpreter designated by LC shall play that role.

The Team Leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters i. e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are maintained at all time among colleagues, etc.
Where practical arrangements are not in place, the Team Leader shall draw the attention of Conference Division or whoever plays that role
When the meeting is over
LA shall send the meeting link received from the Conference Section to interpreters

A day before the meeting or during the pre-meeting tests, roles shall be assigned. Each booth shall appoint a Captain, preferably the most senior staff interpreter or a freelancer if there is no staff in the booth.
The Booth Captain shall create a WhatsApp group with all his team members including the DCP Co-host. S/he should prepare a roster (order of work) in consultation with her/his team members.

The Booth Captain is the interface between her/his booth and the DCP Co-host and Host. S/he shall report any challenge brought to her/his attention or that s/he may have observed and that may adversely affect the smooth performance of her/his colleagues.

Each booth shall agree on their preferences as far as symbols are concerned ( : 5 min more; !: end your sentence;  handover) or messages (5 min before handover: “GET READY” and when it is time: “REPREND” or “GO”)
Upon handing over, the Interpreter that was “on” shall listen to the one who has taken over, from his/her second device, to ensure that the transition is going smoothly

Interpreters’ Co-host is designated by the Host (organiser of the meeting) in consultation with DCP. S/he is the interface between the three Booth Captains and the Host. S/he shall perform sound check by listening to the various booths and confirming that the sound is loud and clear before the meeting starts. When participants log on, another sound check shall be carried out with them to ensure that they are good to go.
When the meeting is over
On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare the Language Schedule and email it to line managers, staff interpreters and translators and other DCP Divisions
Once notified of a meeting outside their duty station, staff interpreters shall make their TVM requests on ECOLINK

On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare ticket requests and send it back to him/her for proper verification. The ticket request shall be sent to DCP who shall sign and forward it to Travel Unit for issuance of tickets.

Should ECOWAS rent a mobile SIE from a supplier, it shall be installed no later than the eve of the meeting, shall be funtional and placed in a position where a clear view of the high table/projection screen is provided. In addition, it shall be compatible with the virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms.

The SIE shall be compatible with virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms. ITS/the service provider shall ensure that the SIE is fitted with required interface and cables, logged on to the interpretation platform and ready for use by interpreters.

LC shall appoint the Team Leader once the team is confirmed. The most senior staff interpreter shall be the de facto Team Leader. In event that there is no staff interpreter on the team, a freelance interpreter designated by LC shall play that role.

The Team Leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters i.e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are maintained at all time among colleagues, etc. In addition, at the time of confirming the offer, the LC/Team Leader shall inform interpreters of any possible requirements to meet in respect of the virtual meeting platform to be used.
When the meeting is over
LC obtains confirmation and contact details of SIE provider from DCP
LC/Team Leader receives/confirms details of designated driver
LC sets time for sound check ahead of meeting

Upon installation, the SIE provider shall test the equipment and ensure that it is working. In respect of hybrid meetings, upon installing the required interface and cables, ITS shall logging on to the interpretation platform and do a preliminary sound check to ensure that it is working

LC with staff interpreters ensure component checks (size and position of booths, booth labels, fans, booth lamps, screen, extension boxes, hybrid interpretation platform interface and cable, swivel chairs, writing materials and stationary, adjoining conducive waiting area for interpreters) and verification of the installation specifications with Conference Division/the technicians
When all physical and technical requirements are checked and deemed satisfactory

Interpreters shall present themselves at the venue of their assigned meetings at least 1 hour to the commencement of the meeting. Sound check shall be done under the Team Leader's supervision to confirm that the SIE is working.
Check that input and output sound are clear
Check that relay is functional and that interpreters master the use of consoles
Check that there is direct sound to the interpreters from the roving mics
Check language settings on consoles and receivers Check sound from the interpretation platform
When all audio parameters are good

Interpreters shall present themselves at the venue of their assigned meetings at least 1 hour to the commencement of the meeting. Sound check shall be done under the Team Leader's supervision to confirm that all the sound parameters are functional. Sound check shall be done concurrently with that of the interpretation platform. A simulation of participants use of audio on the interpretation platform shall also be done.
Check that input and output sound are clear
Check that relay is functional and that interpreters master the use of consoles
Check that there is direct sound to the interpreters from the roving mics
Check language settings on consoles and receivers
Check sound from the interpretation platform
The participants' audio check shall also be done by LC before any audio output is disseminated
When all audio parameters are good
Sound check shall be led by DCP Cohost
LC shall instruct LA to inform colleague interpreters of the start time of sound check
Check that input and output sound are clear
DCP Co-host shall try to resolve challenges that occur and report those beyond his/her control to LC
In the event of serious difficulties that cannot be resolved at their level LC shall inform DCP for appropriate action to be taken by ITS
The participants' audio check shall also be done by LC before any audio output is disseminated
When all audio parameters are good

LC shall ensure that all payment requirements are met in due time. To that effect, LC shall instruct LA to give all relevant information on the payment process to freelance interpreters (and SIE providers) ahead of time and ensure that all relevant supporting documents are submitted in line with ECOWAS financial procedures.

For physical meetings, LA shall ensure early submission of copies of signed contracts and other supporting documents by freelance interpreters (and SIE provider’s endorsed proforma invoice) to the Protocol Assistant (PA) or Conference Assistant (CA) on the meeting. The latter in turn shall submit them to the Finance Directorate for timely and hitch-free payment.

When freelance language professionals are not paid at the venue of the meeting or if it is a virtual meeting, LC shall instruct LA to prepare a cover memo with relevant documents for DCP’s attention for processing
LC shall instruct LA to follow up outstanding payments. LC shall draw DCP’s attention to delays and give relevant information to language service providers concerned.
When all payments including bank transfers to freelance interpreters (and SIE providers)vare made

Team Leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the Client Directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups, and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.
Team Leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
LC shall submit feedback forms to DCP when there are issues requiring DCP’s attention.
When corrective action is taken if need be

PPO Protocol receives approved request from client/superior officer/DCP and confirms validity and responds to client accordingly, PPO Protocol assigns request to Protocol Assistant/Logistics and Reception Officer
Logistics and Reception Officer/Protocol Assistant confirms destination, fills transport request form(s), obtains endorsement from General Admin Directorate and submits to Transport Unit
Actor: Logistics and Receprion Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Logistics and Reception Officer/Protocol Assistant confirms pick up point and departure time to client
Actor: Logistics and Receprion Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Logistics and Reception Officer/Protocol Assistant confirms with transport unit regarding driver(s) and vehicle(s) scheduled for the assignment and contacts assigned driver
Logistics and Reception Officer/Protocol Assistant confirms driver and vehicle readiness to carry out the assignment on eve of assignment
Actor: Logistics and Receprion Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Logistics and Reception Officer/Protocol Assistant briefs supervisor on status of implementation
Actor: Logistics and Receprion Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Delegation is satisfactorily transported or assignment completed
Actor: Logistics and Receprion Officer, Protocol Assistant

Description: Assignment completion is reported to supervisor
Consular Officer prepares Note Verbale and offers guided advice where required
Note Verbale is submitted to Consulate and appointment scheduled and communicated to client
Client(s) show(s) up as appointed Client picks up passport with visa from the Embassy, Consulate/issuing office
Update to supervisor Assignment completed
Assignment is reported to supervisor
PPO Protocol receives memo from DCP notifying the event. PPO Protocol convenes planning meeting with Consularteam.
PPO Protocol contacts counterpart in the Diplomatic Mission to confirm date and schedule for familiarisation visit and dress rehearsal

During dress rehearsal, PPO Protocol and team receive Heads of Mission observing all protocols with regard to accreditation including the following activities: - name - pronounciations, - arrival routes - walk through red carpet - briefing room - accreditation ceremony and departure
On accreditation day, Protocol Officers ensure accreditation venue is duly set up with required paraphenalia and insignia. Final inspection is carried out 1 hour before commencement of event.
Head of Protocol receives the designates in order of arrival and conducts briefing
PPO Protocol informs Protocol Officer attached to President of the Commission of arrival of designates. President is escorted to venue of accreditation by the Protocol Officer to the President.
Respecting order of arrival, the PPO Protocol leads One Heads of Mission, to the accreditation hall for accreditation ceremony to be conducted. PPO Protocol moderates accreditation ceremony.
Each Ambassador/Head of Mission is accompanied to his/her vehicle for departure
PPO Protocol reports completion of a‚ssignment to DCP
PPO Protocol receives instruction to prepare for Diplomatic Cocktail and convenes planning meeting

PPO prepares draft memo for DCP proposing tentative date for Reception to President. Memo shall be accompanied by draft programme, discussion guide, draft budget, proposed guest list, draft input to the President’s speech and all supporting documents
Upon receipt of approval, PPOProtocol assigns responsibilities to Protocol Personnel (arrangement for venue, catering, invitation cards, etc.)
On request of PPO Protocol through DCP, invitation cards are obtained from General Administration and dispatched to guests

Designated Protocol Personnel receives acknowledgements, regrets, assurances and other feedba ck from invitees and prepares final guest list of confirmed attendance for transmission to Management through DCP
PPO Protocol, on eve of the event convenes final planning meeting and briefs the President appropriately

PPO Protocol invites DCP and Supervising Commissioner forfinal inspection no later than 4 hours before commencement time of event. Protocol Personnel mount their duty posts no later than 2 hours before event.

Protocol Personnel at the entrance verifies validity of invitation. PPO Protocol receives guests and ushers guest to accreditation table. Protocol Personnel receives invitation card and marks register. PPO Protocol ushers guest to President of the Commission. PPO Protocol anchors the event as the case may be. Protocol Personnel are strategically stationed throughout the event.
Designated Protocol Personnel organise departure of guests

PPO Protocol is notified in a memo about arrival, pick-up and temporary accommodation, in the hotel (specifying duration of stay), of new staff of the Commission. PPO Protocol assigns responsibility to the Logistics & Reception, Protocol Personnel.

Protocol Personnel notifies transportation unit and hotel in preparation for assignment. Verify ticket with travel unit. Protocol Personnel receives the new staff at the airport and takes him/her to the hotel for accommodation. Protocol Assistant aranges for the staff to be brought from the hotel to HR. Protocol presents a letter of introduction to the designated accommodation facility indicating Commission’s responsibility, date of arrival, name of staff and duration of stay.
Airport reception on arrival of new staff is carried out
Protocol Officer briefs new staff on arrangement for conveyance of the latter for documentation
New staff is handed over to HR
Logistics and Reception Officer, Protocol Assistant briefs PPO Protocol on completion of assignment

PPO Protocol receives request for clearing of consignment and assigns same to Privileges and Immunity Unit who prepares Note Verbale addressed to MFA in quadruplicate copies. Having each of the necessary documents attached P&I personnel submits Note Verbale to MFA.
Note Verbale is submitted to MFA
Response is received from MFA and submitted at the Customs Office Approved document comes out after 4 working days
Payment is made to courier company for delivery of approved customs duty documents to the Customs Office at the Port Office
Copy of approved duty waiver is handed over to the clearing agent/Client
Cleared goods are delivered at the ECOWAS Commission. Privileges and Immunities Unit and client confirm safe delivery of cleared items.
Assignment completed
P&I Personnel briefs PPO Protocol on completion of assignment

Protocol Division receives memo from PAPS requesting participation of at least 2 Protocol Officers on Election Observation Mission as well as budget and comprehensive list of participants on the mission and the approved memo by the President, as attachment

• Protocol Personnel (PP) arrive mission and carry out environmental survelance • PP hold logistic meeting with service providers • PP inspect hotels and mission secretariat • PP draft Arrival, Departure, Accommodation and Transport tables • PP establishs contacts with the office of the Resident Rep and/or ECOWAS National unit through Note verbales to receive the HOM and delegation, state escort and security and airport assistance • Medical contingencies, SIM card procurement, briefing room and secretaries are confirmed

Driver verification, noting the following: - Condition of tyres and spare- Fire extinguisher - Caution sign Suitability for the terrain of deployment Working headlamps  and trafficators, etc. PP brief drivers on recurrent questions: - When and how a driver can vote, general code of conduct and working hours PP present the vehicle list to PAPS for insertion into the deployment list and allocation to observers Observers are informed to pick daily updates at the protocol desks at the secretariat

The PP ensures that the accountant or designated payment office(r) i.e. ECOBANK, has an up-to-date list of arrived election observers with the necessary supporting documents (passport data page and stamped page, ticket and boarding passes) for payment processing. Payment is immediately commenced for delegates especially those traveling to the interior/distant regions.

Before departure, the Protocol Officer and the service provider run final checks on the vehicles, with the list. Emphasis is on vehicle readiness and fueling. The PP labels all vehicles, according to regions and ensures EOM stickers are correctly placed.

Protocol prepares the Head of Mission for election observation by briefing him or her on the excercise. Protocol arranges convoy for departure, remains in contact with the situation room. Protocol monitors airport and border developments, particularly confirms tickets, and note flight changes. Protocol updates and pastes departure schedule at protocol desks and secretariat. Protocol personally reviews the departure plan of the Head of Mission with his team. Protocol reconfirms delegate drop-off plans with hotel shuttle or service provider and other coordination vehicles.

Protocol reviews bills and signs off with service providers for payment processing. Protocol arranges departure of ECOWAS team and announces departure plans to the delegates during the post-election debriefing. Protocol coordinates the departure of both observers and the ECOWAS team, to the airport and ensures all departure formalities are completed.
Protocol team submits mission report within 3 days of return to base. Assignment completed
Language Assistant (LA) receives translation requests and administers the translation analysis checklist
Language Assistant forwards translation requests to Revisor/Senior Translator with copy to Language Coordinator
Revisor/Senior Translator assigns translation
Translator reviews document and commences translation
Revisor/Senior Translator, after consultation with LC, may decide to assign documents and resources to freelance translators subject to DCP's approval
Documents assigned and processed
DCP approves the recruitment of freelancers
LC requests from Language Assistant availability of freelance translators for certain number of pages to be translated
Language Assistant sends document to the confirmed freelance translator(s) specifying required deadline
Freelance translator returns translated document with the invoice to LA
LA sends translated document to Revisor/Senior Translator for review and onward transmission to client
Language Assistant initiates payment process
Documents assigned and processed
Translator finalises document
Translated document sent to Revisor/Senior Translator
Revisor/Senior Translator reviews document and sends back to Language Assistant with copy to LC
Language Assistant forwards translated document to client and requests for feedback
Client acknowledges receipt of document and gives feedback
Language Division receives feedback and acts on it
Documents assigned and processed
Revisor/Senior Translato puts forward names of translators
Language Coordinator reviews and confirms nominations for in-house translation team
Language Coordinator submits the proposed list to Director
Director forwards the proposed list to Conference Division for necessary arrangements and consolidation
Director submits the consolidated list of Commission's delegation to Management for approval
Each staff translator initiates TVM process on ECOLink where necessary
Translation team is available
Language Coordinator directs Language Assistant to check availability of freelance translators for the period
Language Assistant sends availability request to freelance translators
Language Assistant receives confirmation of availability and notifies Language Coordinator
Language Coordinator discusses and agrees on team of freelance translators with Director
Language Coordinator instructs Language Assistant to confirm freelance translators and proceed with issuance and signing of their Contracts
On instruction of Language Coordinator, Language Assistant completes table for tickets and for onward transmission to Director
Language Coordinator communicates translation team to Conference Division for necessary arrangements
Translation team is available
Language Assistant, on instruction of Language Coordinator sends request to destination language Revisor
Revisor assigns translation to translator(s)
Translators return translated documents to Revisor
Revisor collates, verifies and sends translated document to the meeting coordinator/focal person
Revisor ensures that documents are available in the different languages before adoption and sends to drafting team focal person/Ag LC/Director Conference
Translated documents available
Language Coordinator receives amendments made by the meeting to the document
Language Coordinator assigns documents for alignment
Revisor submits aligned document to Language Coordinator
Meeting Coordinator sends adopted document for final alignment
Language Coordinator sends final aligned document to meeting coordinator/focal person
Meeting Coordinator sends adopted document for final alignment
Language Coordinator sends final aligned document to meeting coordinator/focal person
Meeting documents finalised
Language Coordinator forwards a copy of the contract to recruited freelance translators for signing
Freelance translators sign contract and return same to Language Coordinator through Language Assistant
Language Assistant acknowledges receipt of signed contracts
Language Coordinator forwards the signed contract of recruited freelance translators to the Protocol Officer/DCP representative at the meeting/Client Directorate
Should payment be made during the meeting, Protocol Officer/DCP representative at the meeting/Client Directorate submits the contract of the recruited Freelance Translators to Accountant for payment
If payment is to be made by Directorate of Finance at the Commission, Language Coordinator instructs Language Assistant to prepare the necessary paperwork
Freelance translators’ payment ready
